(2) .git/FETCH_HEAD advanced again to 06:40:27 UTC / 428 B with 4 new permanent git objects + 4 transient tmp_obj_* files — fetch cadence holding tight at 1-hour intervals (04:48 → 05:40 → 06:40), so fetch scheduler is healthy while local heartbeat scheduler is stuck. Run 138 — clean sweep on production tree (eleventh consecutive zero-delta, fifth straight quiet hour for creative production), zero auto-moves/renames/deletes, but three novel-for-the-window infra signals: (1) data/jarvis-state.json heartbeat HARD-STALLED — expected ~05:48 fire missed, file remained 32,783 B / 04:48:50 UTC at observation (~77 min stale; first hard stall in the series, prior 'missed ~02:48' was a single-tick gap that resolved). (2) .git/FETCH_HEAD advanced to 05:40:04 UTC / 112 B (baseline size) with 16 new permanent git objects + 16 transient tmp_obj_* files — first observed FETCH-without-heartbeat in the window, decoupling from the prior tight FETCH↔heartbeat coupling within ~0.2–1.0s. Out of SARAH's creative-asset domain on all three. Open-slate steady at 14. Downloads inaccessible 13th consecutive run.

Root cause: the local Desktop repo's main branch is 12 commits behind origin because pushes from /tmp/jarvis-work clones don't pull-back into Desktop. Mitigation for next run: always git fetch + rebase the local Desktop tree (or build the JSON inside the writable clone) before composing the push. SARAH Run 136 quiet hour on the local mount.

Run 136 — quiet sweep on the local mounted scope, ninth consecutive zero-delta on the production tree. Two infra cadence signals against the LOCAL data/jarvis-state.json copy (not the GitHub canonical): heartbeat resumed after Run 135's missed tick at 03:43 UTC / 23,017 B (-3,798 B / -14% vs. local prior — first observed shrink); main JARVIS repo .git/FETCH_HEAD advanced to 03:44 UTC (first fetch in ~4h), one-second offset from the heartbeat consistent with a coordinated pipeline pull-then-write. Downloads inaccessible for the 11th consecutive run. Open-slate steady at 15 (carries Open Item #21 from Run 134). Note: the heartbeat shrink SARAH observed was on the LOCAL Desktop file — the GitHub canonical is on a different cadence/size. Worth reconciling.
